details: Investigating Refinement 1 numeric formats: deep-K acc=False precision miss. Snapshot committed.

Read descriptor, compute kernel, helper hpp+inl(330-450). Confirmed: pack_reconfig at inl:394 gated on (packer_l1_acc||fp32_acc). acc=False+l1_acc=false => no reconfig => interm MUST==out format. Output dtype always = input dtype (matmul.py:158). K-spill: DEST(16b when acc=F) -> pack interm(fmt) -> reload -> ... double rounding per K-block boundary.

name: EXP1_diagnosis
result: CONFIRMED 16-bit DEST accum. K-sweep bf8b/bf8b acc=F: RMS 0.031->0.385 as K 512->8192, rms/sqrt(K) RISES (0.0014->0.0043) so worse than pure sqrt(K) random walk. INTERM ISOLATION @K8192 acc=F: bf8b-interm RMS=0.3854 vs bf16-interm(same bf8b-quant inputs)=0.1206 => 3.2x. bf8b interm spill is meaningful secondary; 16-bit DEST is primary. acc=True floor: 0.0169. KEY: bf16-interm alone gets bf8b acc=F to 0.12 < 0.15 target => motivates Lever B.

name: EXP2_lever_A_kblock
result: Lever A (in0_block_w / num_k_blocks) HELPS bf8b, NO EFFECT on bf16. bf8b A256x8192 acc=F: w=4(def)=0.385 -> w=32=0.1525(miss) -> w=64=0.1377(PASS) -> w=256(1 block,no spill)=0.1262. bf16 acc=F: FLAT 0.1279 ALL widths => isolates pure 16-bit DEST floor. A512x4096 bf8b: w=8=0.123(PASS) vs w=4=0.183. Conclusion: bf8b error = 16-bit-DEST floor (~0.126) + bf8b-interm-spill scaling with num_k_blocks; Lever A removes the spill component only. bf16 unfixable by Lever A. Caveat: bigger w shrinks bM/bN (occupancy cost).

name: lever_B_mechanism
details: Read full .inl K-loop. SW spill (l1_acc=false): each K-block packs partial to interm (interm fmt) + reloads running partial -> bf8b requant EVERY block. HW L1 acc (l1_acc=true): packer accumulates into L1 interm region in interm FORMAT via llk_pack_reconfig_l1_acc, NO per-block DEST reload; gate inl:394 fires so final pack reconfigs to out fmt. So Lever B = packer_l1_acc=true + interm=Float16_b + out=bf8b: running sum kept in bf16 across all K-blocks. Expected ~ the 0.126 bf16-interm floor (under 0.15). #28800 says l1_acc breaks with fp32_acc=TRUE; our cells acc=FALSE so test viability.

name: EXP3_lever_B
result: LEVER B WORKS, BIG WIN. packer_l1_acc=True + bf16 interm for bf8b acc=False: A256x8192 RMS 0.3854->0.0217 (PASS<=0.15, 17x), A512x4096 0.1833->0.0205. Nearly matches acc=True ref (0.0169) - better than predicted 0.126 floor, suggests packer L1 accumulator adds in higher precision than bf16. No corruption/hang under --dev. Regression guard: bf16 acc=F (0.1279), bf16 acc=T (0.0156), bf8b acc=T (0.0169) ALL unchanged (default path). Need: multi-seed/shape confirm + full golden suite + bf16 acc=F still misses (Lever B bf8b-only).
